title: Imagery
summary: We have a new lineup of amazing images in our image bank to make SEB stand out. We want to show an elevated reality and create a distinctive SEB look. Images should reflect SEBs attention to detail and quality. In accordance with this, our shadows are cold, and our highlights are warm to represent our professionalism and human values.

columns.title: How we think
columns.content: Choosing images takes a lot of consideration. Try to create tension between the copy and the image. Don't let the image show the same thing as the copy – instead, tell a story with the combination of both.

columns.title: How we shoot
columns.content: Consider every part of the image. What’s present in the fore- and background. Consider the color palette of the image. Too many colors distract from the subject. A simple color palette adds to the perception of the image. For example, blue shadows and orange mid- and high tones.

columns.text: When choosing images of urban environments always incorporate some kind of greenery. Parks, trees, hanging gardens. Stick to interesting natural lighting that enhances architecture. Light play and shadows can be as powerful as the subject itself. Shooting during sunrise and sundown usually brings out interesting lighting. Always plan ahead with natural light in mind.

We strive to find images of nature that has natural colours and some kind of dramatic component. Big landscapes and aerial shots – preferably with a few layers to get a good scene of scale. Depending on the subject, images can span from Nordic nature to environments from around the world.
A consistency in images help us to build brand recognition. When zooming in to the extremes of our new images you still see and feel that it’s the same look and brand.
It's important to consider each element you use.  Using whitespace as much as possible is a great way to create a balanced and visually pleasing design.
The message is everything. Colors can be used to highlight important information, but in most cases, they distract from what we’re trying to communicate.
The purpose is to elevate the message, in every way we can.
All of our images can be found in our Media Bank. Here you'll find images tagged with different keywords to easily find the one you are looking for.
Important! Using images outside our Media Bank can result in fines, so keep to the images we have licensed.
Don'tNever use low quality or low resolution photographs.
DoPortray people in a natural way.
DoMake sure that the photographs have subtle colors.
Don'tAvoid bright and bold colours in the images.
DoCrop images so that they fit your format.
Don'tMake sure that you don't crop out any key parts of the image.

columns.caption: Shoot real-life situations — Having a coffee, meeting a colleague, working, eating lunch, etc. We want to achieve a warm and approachable feeling towards SEB, so have fun – show some personality!

Find or create interesting lighting to add some further depth to the image. Bring geometric shapes into the equation and let the light do the work for you.  Shooting when the sun is at its extreme often gives very interesting light play.

Keep one side of the image clean and preferably dark to make the legibility as good as possible when adding text.  Add a sense of depth to the image by using objects or persons in the immediate foreground. These can be in or out of focus.

Consider the styling of the model to make them feel incorporated into the environment.   For a more premium and cohesive look, consider the amount of color in the frame. Try to stay within the same spectrum of color as our other images.  Are you shooting a woman in a red elevator, consider styling the person with red to make the whole image feel more cohesive.
